NICHE: Shariah-compliant real-asset tokenization — Saudi first chapter, chapter-scalable across the GCC
THESIS: Agentic origination, structuring, servicing, and stewardship of real-world asset tokenization under Shariah-compliant structures. First binding substrate: Grade-A income-generating Saudi real estate.
VISION: A sovereign tokenization centre that converts serious counterparties from uncertainty into governed tokenization programs, without extractive rent and without pretending regulatory or banking capability not yet earned.
MISSION: Win mandates, structure programs, and operate the full lifecycle (issuance → servicing → stewardship) for tokenized real-world assets in the GCC, starting in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia.
ONE_PARAGRAPH_DESCRIPTION: Agentz is a portfolio-minority JV with Al Wasil Group that operates as the tokenization-operating-company lane of the broader ecosystem. The outward brand is Agentz (agentz.cloud); the public product-face is The Token Centre by AGENTZ. Internal entity, filesystem, contracts, and data room retain AATC. The first chapter is Saudi Arabia (Agentz-KSA). Three sellable motions (B2C retail trust distribution, B2B certification + infrastructure, B2G strategic partnership) are unified by a Halal Tokenization Standard and governed by an independent Sharia Supervisory Board once seated.
IDENTITY_SUMMARY: Agentz is the outward tokenization-operating brand; AATC is the internal entity name and category acronym; Agentz-KSA is the Saudi first chapter.
SOVEREIGNTY_BOUNDARY_SUMMARY: Portfolio-minority JV with Al Wasil Group, 45/45/10 cap table, authority under SHA reserved-matters regime. K2 applies at the DAC / framework IP layer; legal-entity layer is governed by the SHA, not unilateral founder signature.
CONSTITUTIONAL_NOTE: η = 0 at the DAC layer — fees exist on catalytic work (origination, structuring, issuance, servicing, stewardship), not on access or extraction. All outward commercial language is bounded by stakeholders/EXTERNAL_WORDING_REGISTER_2026_04_20.md until counsel upgrades the tier. No claim of live licence, live SSB certification, or live platform operation until receipts exist.
SUCCESS_CONDITION: First SSB-certified Agentz-KSA tokenization issuance closed on a real Saudi asset (SA_001 Riyadh Grade-A Office or equivalent), with recurring servicing fees flowing and documented rights-mapping + wrapper-viability workflow reproducible for asset #2.
FOUNDER_PUBKEY: TBD — to be set when organism-side K2 signer for Agentz is formally designated (expected at or after SHA close)
SUBSTRATE_DEFINITION: Real-world assets with durable economic rights currently trapped in opaque, illiquid, or jurisdiction-locked ownership structures. First binding substrate: Grade-A income-generating Saudi real estate (SA_001 hypothesis). Adjacent substrates in scope for later chapters: other income-generating real estate classes, commodities with title provenance, and reserve-collateral surfaces.
SUBSTRATE_SUMMARY: Real-world assets with durable economic rights that need a Shariah-compliant, legally defensible, and operationally governed path to divisible digital ownership. First asset class: Saudi Grade-A income-generating real estate.
SUBSTRATE_UPLINK_SUMMARY: Saudi income-generating real estate, scaling to GCC real-asset classes.
ENTROPY_SOURCES: (1) Real-estate cap-rate indices + comparable-transaction data (Knight Frank, JLL, local broker feeds); (2) counterparty dialogue logs and commitment signals captured in stakeholders/; (3) Saudi regulatory updates (SAMA, CMA, REDF); (4) SSB rulings and comparable precedent; (5) independent valuer reports; (6) asset-level income feeds once a live mandate exists.
BASELINE_MODEL: The counterparty default is selling, financing, or leasing the asset through conventional channels without tokenization. Baseline yield benchmarked against local private-placement / Murabaha-alternative yields. Agentz alpha is measured net of that default.
MISMATCH_1: Shariah-compliance ambiguity — counterparties want tokenization without a defensible Shariah ruling, leaving them exposed.
MISMATCH_2: Rights-mapping gap — Saudi title, beneficial-rights, and common-area governance logic is not yet encoded in tokenization wrappers.
MISMATCH_3: Operational-discipline gap — most tokenization narratives end at issuance; Agentz operates lifecycle servicing, stewardship, and reporting.
MISMATCH_4: Regulatory-pathway uncertainty — whether tokenized SPV beneficial interests escape full securities regulation under current Saudi law is unresolved; Agentz absorbs that legal work.
HAZARD_TEMPLATE: Per-asset wrapper viability under Saudi securities law; counterparty commitment durability across SHA / mandate stages; SSB approval timing and scope; independent-valuer disagreement; regulatory-posture shift between signing and closing.
HAZARD_SUMMARY: Wrapper / counterparty / SSB / valuer / regulator-timing hazards compound across the mandate lifecycle.
HAZARD_UPLINK_SUMMARY: Wrapper + counterparty + SSB + valuer + regulator hazards.
HAZARD_1: Wrapper-viability: can tokenized SPV beneficial interests avoid full securities regulation under current Saudi law? — first counsel question queued.
HAZARD_2: Counterparty durability: named counterparties (Fawaz Bashraheel Holding, Retal, NHC, ROSHN, Red Sea Global) may drop between LOI and mandate close.
HAZARD_3: SSB-seating delay: until the SSB is seated, Shariah-compliance claims are Interpretive-tier, not Empirical.
HAZARD_4: Regulatory drift: SAMA / CMA / REDF posture on tokenization may shift mid-mandate; mandates must include regulator-change-of-law clauses.
ALPHA_MODEL: Hybrid fee stack per mandate: (1) fixed discovery fee; (2) milestone structuring fee; (3) issuance fee; (4) recurring servicing and stewardship revenue (SaaS-like recurring stream). Not rent — each fee binds to an identifiable catalytic unit of work. η = 0 at the DAC layer.
ALPHA_DESCRIPTION: Agentz catalyzes the conversion of illiquid, opaque, jurisdiction-locked assets into Shariah-compliant, legally defensible, operationally governed tokenized programs. Each fee maps to a measurable catalytic output: a diagnostic, a structured wrapper, a live issuance, or recurring servicing of a live token.
ALPHA_SUMMARY: Discovery + structuring + issuance + recurring servicing & stewardship fees, bound to catalytic units of work.
ALPHA_UPLINK_SUMMARY: Discovery → structuring → issuance → recurring servicing & stewardship.
MEMBRANE_RULE_1: Outward brand = AGENTZ. Outward domain = agentz.cloud. Outward product-face = The Token Centre by AGENTZ. Internal entity, filesystem, contracts, and data room retain AATC.
MEMBRANE_RULE_2: K2 signing required for framework-IP actions; SHA reserved-matters govern legal-entity actions; neither collapses into the other.
MEMBRANE_RULE_3: All outward commercial wording is bounded by stakeholders/EXTERNAL_WORDING_REGISTER_2026_04_20.md until counsel upgrades the tier.
MEMBRANE_RULE_4: Grace Exit (K4): any counterparty may exit their participation with their asset rights intact; Agentz retains no residual claim beyond documented fees already earned.
RECEIPT_SUMMARY: Mandate signed, milestone hit, issuance settled, servicing period closed, dispute filed, dispute resolved.
RECEIPTS_UPLINK_SUMMARY: Mandate / milestone / issuance / servicing / dispute events — each event a receipt.
OPERATIONS_UPLINK_SUMMARY: Outbound wave 1 (5 Saudi targets) → mandate close → SSB engagement → wrapper ruling → first issuance → recurring servicing.
PATHOLOGY_UPLINK_SUMMARY: Substrate hallucination (declaring asset rights not mapped); overbinding (mandate scope exceeding counterparty commitment); runaway catalysis (issuing without SSB / wrapper / regulator alignment); membrane leak (AATC internal names appearing in outward copy); parasitic extraction (fees decoupled from catalytic work).
METRIC_2: Time-to-first-issuance from SHA close (target: Phase 1 + 12 months)
METRIC_3: Recurring servicing revenue / (cumulative discovery + structuring + issuance fees) — measures lifecycle-stickiness
METRIC_4: P-score (Φ × ν) at the Agentz-KSA chapter level; current 0.16 (pre-close)
ECONOMIC_SURFACE_1: Discovery fee — fixed, per-mandate, earned on diagnostic completion.
ECONOMIC_SURFACE_2: Milestone structuring fee — milestone-gated, earned on documented structural deliverables (wrapper design, rights map, SSB submission).
ECONOMIC_SURFACE_3: Issuance fee — earned on live SSB-certified issuance of the token.
ECONOMIC_SURFACE_4: Recurring servicing & stewardship — SaaS-like monthly/quarterly stream, bound to live servicing workload.
